session_summary: Session executed the general-p Cox-Snell b_Sigma derivation (todo/015) aiming to reproduce the empirical non-monotone (A)+(C) value of -0.46, but the magnitude bookkeeping did not close. Marcus's role was to probe the imputation methodology (Amelia vs conjugate data augmentation, burn-in, starting values), catch that the manuscript was speculating beyond what was derived, and spin the unresolved residual into cold-load hand-off plans (todo/017, todo/018) including a dual Claude+GPT-5.5 derivation requirement.

coded_turns.quote: Resume mi-spectral: EXECUTE general-p non-monotone Cox–Snell b_Σ → reach the 4-variate phase-8 −0.46.
coded_turns.note: Detailed cold-start brief: execute general-p b_Sigma to target -0.46, with validation ladder, gates, and gotchas spelled out.

coded_turns.quote: I’m wondering if we should ask chat got 5.5 through API. Found good results with that in an adversarial test
coded_turns.note: Proposes cross-model adversarial check via GPT-5.5 API, citing prior good results; invokes the cross-model verification protocol.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/016; commit ffff008 (GPT-5.5 Layer-3 adversarial check)

coded_turns.note: System task-completion notification for the backgrounded GPT-5.5 adversarial run; routes the result into the session.

coded_turns.quote: Is it all possible that the issue may be exacerbated by using Amelia rather than using conjugacy for a closes forn posterior distribution when sampling imputation
coded_turns.note: Hypothesizes Amelia's bootstrap imputation, not conjugate posterior draws, may be inflating the unexplained magnitude gap.

coded_turns.quote: Does da rely on a package to sample or is the posterior calculated analytically so draws are truly independent?
coded_turns.note: Probes whether da.norm draws are truly independent vs package-sampled, stress-testing the proposed reference engine.

coded_turns.quote: What burnin period are we using etc?
coded_turns.note: Asks for burn-in settings, surfacing MCMC-convergence hygiene the AI had not pinned for the DA chain.

coded_turns.quote: Can you set starting values for the missing imputations?
coded_turns.note: Asks for over-dispersed starts, enabling the multi-start R-hat convergence diagnostic the AI then built into the plan.

coded_turns.quote: we will went to investigate this, but  in a different session. We’ll probably first want a targeted and strategic screener test to see how sensitive conclusions are to using a Bayesianly proper imputation method vs EMB
coded_turns.note: Defers the engine investigation to a separate session, calls for a strategic screener first, orders a cold-loadable markdown.

coded_turns.quote: Give me the prompt for this
coded_turns.note: Requests the cold-start prompt for the deferred engine-sensitivity session.

coded_turns.quote: Now I distrtacted us a little bit. YUou mentioned the below, but I need my memory freshed of what we have learned in this session and why you suggested this
coded_turns.note: Pulls back from the engine tangent to the lavaan-FIML realized-information lead; asks for a session recap and rationale.

coded_turns.quote: Yes please. I'm doing todo/017 currently in a nother session, so don't worry about that
coded_turns.note: Approves the lavaan-FIML re-measurement and confirms the engine axis is being handled in a parallel session.

coded_turns.quote: Yes, let's commit, but this probably means that we are inappropriately speculating in the derivations manuscript
coded_turns.note: Approves the commit while flagging that the manuscript over-speculates beyond what is derived, triggering de-speculation.

coded_turns.quote: yes to this:  Want me to draft the todo/018 reconciliation plan?
coded_turns.note: Approves drafting the todo/018 reconciliation hand-off plan for the unresolved realized-information residual.

coded_turns.quote: Yes write a cold start prompt
coded_turns.note: Requests the cold-start prompt for the todo/018 reconciliation session.

coded_turns.quote: Can we have it derived in two wyas: Both here with claude and thorugh api chat-gpt 5.5?
coded_turns.note: Imposes dual-derivation requirement (Claude + GPT-5.5) on the todo/018 reconciliation, hardening the cross-model gate.

ai_side.evidence: Recommended next step (your call): a clean lavaan-FIML re-measurement of rem ... Want me to run that now, or leave it flagged in todo/016 for later?
ai_side.note: Put the lavaan re-measurement to Marcus as a choice rather than running it silently.

ai_side.evidence: absolute rem came out +0.06/+0.53, opposite sign to the committed lavaan −0.34/−0.46. So the magnitude bookkeeping isn't closed; only the paired info-gap and B_cov are trustworthy.
ai_side.note: Flagged that the committed magnitude result is not trustworthy on its own initiative.

ai_side.evidence: If da.norm ran in the parallel production cluster as-is, the per-worker norm RNG streams wouldn't be explicitly seeded — a reproducibility and cross-worker-independence hazard. I'd set rngseed per worker.
ai_side.note: Self-flagged an RNG-seeding reproducibility hazard in the DA plan.

ai_side.evidence: (i) analytic FIML (current baseline), (ii) proper MI via conjugate da.norm, (iii) proper MI via Amelia — at n=800/1500, and compare both the value and its stability
ai_side.note: Laid out a three-engine comparison design with trade-offs for Marcus to choose.

ai_side.evidence: Given it puts the committed reframe in doubt, I'd lean toward committing the diagnostic now (so the finding is durable) and proceeding to the confirming computation.
ai_side.note: Surfaced the commit-now-vs-wait decision, noting the committed reframe is in doubt.

ai_side.evidence: Pre-existing citation warning: quarto render reports @Tanner01061987 not found in the bib ... it's a live citation-discipline gap worth resolving via the lit-acquisition workflow.
ai_side.note: Flagged a dangling citation as a citation-discipline gap, distinguishing it from its own edits.
